design_philosophy.core_loop: Travel seamlessly through an interconnected world, engage in visible overworld battles, collect melodies from eight Sanctuaries, defeat cosmic threat Giygas
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Subversion of RPG conventions through modern Americana setting, tonal clash between idiosyncratic humor and genuine cosmic horror
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control a party of pre-pubescent kids with psychic powers solving mysteries and saving the world from alien invasion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentionally goofy tone rejecting genre solemnity; modern setting with mundane enemies (taxi cabs, vomit piles, walking nooses) parodying fantasy RPG tropes; seamless world with no overworld map distinction; visible enemies replacing random encounters; targets non-gamers with accessible, humorous presentation

difficulty.continues_system: Return to last save point upon party wipe
difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save points at various locations throughout world
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Visible enemies allow strategic avoidance of dangerous encounters; weaker enemies auto-defeat or flee rather than force engagement; player can manipulate battle order through speed stats and RNG manipulation in advanced play

progression.upgrade_system: Level-up system pseudo-randomly increases character attributes (offense, defense, HP, PP); equipment items provide stat modifications
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight Sanctuaries must be visited in sequence to collect melodies; each area unlocks access to next region; character PSI abilities unlock through level progression
replayability_hooks: RNG manipulation mechanics enable speedrunning strategies
Visible enemy avoidance creates alternate routing possibilities
Extensive post-game content and hidden areas reward exploration
Multiple character build paths through selective leveling

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through seamless interconnected world
Dialogue-driven character development and NPC interactions
Cutscenes advancing plot milestones (party member recruitment, sanctuary visits)
In-game hallucinations and psychological sequences reflecting character states
Americana-themed locations (villages, cities, beaches) grounding fantastical plot
Phone calls from NPCs providing real-time story updates and world context

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve narrative progression milestones; fights against abstract/mundane enemy types (Starman entities, Giygas manifestations) emphasize tonal dissonance; late-game bosses feature phase-based battles with escalating cosmic horror
notable_bosses: Starman Jr. - early game tutorial boss
Giygas - final boss with multiple manifestation phases
Master Belch - swamp area environmental threat
Ness' Nightmare - psychological/internal conflict boss in Magicant

faction_indicators: Chosen Four party members represent different social backgrounds (rural, industrial, royal, foreign)
Enemy types reflect societal anxieties (cops, businessmen, animals, aliens)
danger_signals: Alien presence (Starmen, Giygas influence) corrupting human society
environmental_storytelling: Onett small-town Americana establishing baseline normalcy
Progressive urbanization through Twoson, Threed, Fourside showing societal complexity
Desert regions and Lost Underworld revealing hidden dimensions of reality
Magicant representing collective unconscious and inner psychological space
Final areas showing cosmic scope of threat transcending geography
